name: How Do I Know If I've Hired a Good General Contractor?

step.name: Step 1
step.text: Feeling confident in your general contractor is the key to a low-stress remodel. But how can you be sure you’ve made the right choice before the work even starts? It comes down to checking a few key things.

step.name: Step 2
step.text: First and foremost: licensing and insurance. Don’t just take their word for it. A reputable contractor will eagerly provide their license number and proof of liability insurance. This is non-negotiable and protects you from liability. Next, look at their track record. Ask for references from recent projects that are similar in scope to yours. A good contractor will have a portfolio of finished work and happy clients.

step.name: Step 3
step.text: Pay close attention to their communication style. Are they responsive to your questions? Do they explain the process clearly? A great contractor provides a detailed, itemized contract that outlines the scope, timeline, payment schedule, and process for handling changes. Vague agreements are a major warning sign. Ultimately, you're hiring a project manager, so you need someone who is organized, transparent, and professional from the start.

step.name: Step 4
step.text: If you want to partner with a trusted, experienced, and fully licensed general contractor in the San Francisco Bay Area, call City Dweller, LLC today.

description: How Do I Know If I've Hired a Good General Contractor?
